title: A Lean 4 formalization of a SimpleDNF switching lemma with CNF-dual and explicit layer-schedule infrastructure
description:
(v0.7.0) Gate A realized-code/row-collision obstruction and no-go packaging through S2140, plus Gate B syntactic-terminal class envelopes through the restricted OR-only width-one package S2156, the restricted OR/AND recurrence-width package S2157 (max-OR / sum-AND recurrence width with supplied-width final-tree routing), the disjoint-support AND-compatibility synthesis class S2158 (pairwise-disjoint child variable supports synthesize the AND-list simplicity with no constructor-carried compatibility hypothesis), the numerically-discharged unconditional supplied-width final-tree instances S2160 (coarse ambient threshold discharged by decide on Nat literals at fixed ambients 2^22 and 2^31), and the frontier-local tight-entry consumers S2161 (layer-local gate-count/width ambient obligations with zero-hypothesis instances at 2^20/rounds 2, 2^26/rounds 3, and 2^32/rounds 4, strictly dominating the S2160 instances), all under unchanged t(d,s)=S(d)*(s-1). A curated Lean 4 artifact for a SimpleDNF switching lemma in a canonical decision-tree encoding, together with a CNF-dual bridge, bounded-depth formula bridge infrastructure, explicit and generated layer-schedule certificates, variable-coverage floors for a local cut-free bounded-depth Tait trace system, a parameterized 2h falsified-clause search depth floor with formalized 3x2 tightness, and (v0.5.0) a family Boolean decision-tree depth floor of h*h for the satisfiable h x h pigeonhole function (evasiveness), depth floors under partial-matching restrictions with the (h-s)*h boundary family, the exact star-counting uniform matching-restriction space (star probability (h-s)/h in counting form; the uniform-over-injections space is not formalized), generated good-restriction and simultaneous-collapse machinery under counting side conditions, consistent-subspace and refinement-subspace composition, generated and refined iterated-collapse certificate theorems with the consistent-route satisfiability gap disclosed and closed for the refinement route, decision-tree DNF/CNF re-viewing with automatic next-layer scaffolding, and a schedule-driven automatic many-round collapse whose pinned statement records only stage gate counts, budgets, and star counts, with one concrete finite three-stage demo containing the artifact's first counting-beat-backed stage budget s > 2 (s = 3; the earlier GraphIndexedBridge record carries s = 49 but with a directly supplied good restriction, not a counting beat).

Non-claims: this artifact does not prove P != NP, P = NP, an NP or circuit lower bound, a Frege/PHP lower bound, a proof-size lower bound for any proof system with cut, arbitrary AC0 collapse, arbitrary bounded-depth formula collapse, a general CNF switching lemma independent of the explicit dualization bridge, a PHP switching lemma (Gate A rung 4 remains open), or closure of the frozen-form B4 obligation (the pinned obstruction map is intentionally nonempty). The satisfiable-PHP depth floors are elementary evasiveness/sensitivity results inhabiting the repository's depth-floor statement surface; for p > h the PHP formula is identically false and no positive Boolean depth floor is claimed there. The 2h search floor is a decision-tree bound for the falsified-clause search problem (vacuous for p <= h; non-vacuity witnessed at 3x2) with tightness formalized only at the fixed 3x2 instance. The coverage/trace floors remain structural bounds, linear in the number of PHP variables, on the repository's local cut-free trace system. Per-stage arithmetic beat conditions are supplied side conditions (no single product hypothesis); realized widths of automatically re-viewed gates are budget claims; the concrete scheduled and renormalized instances are single finite instances, not asymptotic families.
